      • Sync is easy to add later if needed
      • Sync users today have an escape hatch

open-meteo-client
  • Tutorial
  • Getting started
  • View page source

Getting started

Pre-alpha caveat

While v0.1.0 is in progress, the only thing you can actually do is call ping(). The snippets below that use today() and friends are a preview of the intended API.

Install

pip install open-meteo-client

Verify the install

from openmeteo import ping

print(ping())
# -> "open-meteo-client 0.0.4: pong"

If that works, the package is healthy.

(Preview, v0.1.0) Your first forecast in five lines

import asyncio
from openmeteo import today  # arriving in v0.1.0

async def main() -> None:
    forecast = await today("Dresden")
    print(f"It's currently {forecast.current.temperature_2m}°C in Dresden.")

asyncio.run(main())

What’s next

  • Browse the How-to guides for specific tasks.

  • See the full public API in the Reference.

  • Read the design explanations if you care about the “why”.
